---
title: "Cross-City Comparable Analysis"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Cross-City Comparable Analysis}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
library(tidycops)
```

## Goal

Use `std_*` fields for apples-to-apples incident comparisons across cities.

## Inspect common field coverage

```{r}
list_common_incident_fields(min_cities = 8)
```

## Pull comparable data

```{r eval = FALSE}
sf <- get_standardized_incidents(
  city = "san_francisco",
  last_n_days = 14,
  output = "comparable",
  limit = 5000
)

chi <- get_standardized_incidents(
  city = "chicago",
  last_n_days = 14,
  output = "comparable",
  limit = 5000
)
```

## Bind and summarize

```{r eval = FALSE}
library(dplyr)

combined <- bind_rows(sf, chi)

combined |>
  count(std_city, std_offense_category, sort = TRUE)
```

## Filter tips

```{r eval = FALSE}
filtered <- get_standardized_incidents(
  city = "cincinnati",
  last_n_days = 30,
  offense_category = "property crime",
  beat = "D3B3",
  output = "comparable",
  limit = 5000
)
```

## Coverage caveats

Always inspect `list_incident_sources()` before comparison because some cities
publish rolling windows or scope-limited feeds.
